items.title: Mum claims primary school bullies broke son’s arm, Kedah Education Dept investigating
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 24 — The Kedah Education Department (JPN) is investigating the alleged bullying of a primary school pupil in the state, following media reports on the matter yesterday. State education director Abdul Rahim Mat said the department views the allegations seriously and guaranteed a transparent and thorough investigation to ensure justice for the victim. Media reports previously highlighted that a nine-year-old boy was allegedly bullied in three separate incidents at a primary school in the Kubang Pasu district.

items.title: 'They were calling for help,' teen recounts drowning tragedy of two brothers
items.summary: [[nid:1447337]] In the 10.56am incident, Muhammad Amir Ashraf Mohd Zul-Zunaidi, 15, and his 14-year-old brother Muhammad Aidil Amsyar Mohd Zul-Zunaidi, both students of SMKA Durian Guling, Marang, were swept away by strong currents. Afi Naashif said he had gone to the river with his friend Muhammad Farish Iqbal Cik Mohanarunasdi, 14, and the two brothers for a swim. I managed to grab Amir Ashraf's hand while Farish managed to grab Aidil Amsyar's hand.

items.title: Teen's body found at riverbed, search for younger brother continues
items.summary: DUNGUN: The body of 15-year-old Muhammad Amir Ashraf Mohd Zul-Zunaidi, who was feared drowned while bathing with his younger brother in Sungai Kampung Beril near Paka, was found this evening. Acting district police chief Deputy Superintendent Jumaidy Bah Chong Weh said the body was recovered by the Chukai Fire and Rescue Department's water rescue team at 3.47pm. Jumaidy said a search and rescue operation involving 37 personnel from the Fire and Rescue Department, police and villagers was launched immediately after authorities received reports of the incident.

items.title: Israeli fire kills parents and their infant in Gaza, medics say
items.summary: GAZA: An Israeli airstrike on an apartment in a refugee camp in central Gaza on Sunday left three people dead, including a six-month-old child, health officials said. The October ceasefire, brokered by US President Donald Trump, has failed to halt Israeli attacks in Gaza, with Israel and Hamas deadlocked in indirect talks over the resistance group's disarmament. Four Israeli soldiers have been killed by fighters during the same period, according to the Israeli military.

items.title: Traffic builds up as Aidiladha holidaymakers return home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic congestion began building up on several major highways nationwide as of 5pm today as Malaysians travelled during the Aidiladha holiday weekend, with slow-moving traffic reported along the eastbound Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway (KLK). The Malaysian Highway Authority (MHA) said traffic was moving slowly from Gombak towards Bentong, including stretches near Bukit Tinggi and Bentong on the KLK. "Slow-moving traffic was also reported on the North-South Expressway (PLUS) northbound from Pedas Linggi towards Port Dickson, as well as after the Sungai Besi Toll Plaza heading towards Kuala Lumpur city centre.

items.title: Malaysian among 20 rescued after building collapse in Philippines
items.summary: MANILA: A 51-year-old Malaysian national is among more than 20 people rescued after a multi-storey building under construction collapsed in the Angeles City on Sunday, reports said. Angeles city information officer Jay Pelayo told Reuters that the Malaysian was staying in a nearby budget hotel that was damaged when the concrete structure came down. Regional Fire Protection Bureau information officer Maria Leah Sajili said five people were confirmed trapped, two of whom were in contact with rescuers, while 18 more construction workers on duty at the time had not yet been accounted for.

items.title: Asked about Pandan candidacy for GE16, Tengku Zafrul says decision rests with PKR leadership
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz today said PKR’s leadership will decide whether or not he contests the Pandan parliamentary seat in the 16th general election (GE16). The prime minister’s senior political adviser said the matter was up to PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, The Star reported. Tengku Zafrul said he has been working closely with the Ampang Jaya Municipal Council to address issues affecting residents in Pandan.

items.title: Wet and stormy weather for Selangor, Johor, Sabah, Sarawak and peninsular east coast tonight
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) today issued a thunderstorm warning for several states across peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak until 7pm. MetMalaysia said the warning involves areas in Perak, Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang, Selangor, Negeri Sembilan and Johor. The warning in Kelantan covers Tumpat, Pasir Mas, Kota Bharu, Bachok, Machang and Pasir Puteh.

items.title: Maran PKR gears up for Luit, Kuala Sentul contests ahead of GE16
items.summary: MARAN: The Maran PKR division has already begun laying the groundwork to wrest the Luit and Kuala Sentul state seats, anticipating multi-cornered contests in the 16th General Election (GE16). Its division chief, Michael Sia Wei Lung, said this follows political developments in Negri Sembilan, as well as Barisan Nasional's announcement in Johor that it will contest all 56 seats in the upcoming state election. He said, during the recent Pakatan Harapan convention in Johor, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is PKR president and PH chairman, had urged members to prepare for the possibility of a snap election with multi-cornered contests.

items.title: Putrajaya reports no new deaths from Covid-19 this year as Singapore battles fresh wave
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — The current Covid-19 situation in Malaysia remains stable and has shown a significant downward trend compared to last year, with no deaths recorded so far this year. The Ministry of Health (MOH), in a statement today, said although neighbouring countries have recently reported an increase in cases, the number of Covid-19 cases in the country up to Epidemiological Week (EW) 19/2026 had declined by 12.6 per cent (9,682 cases) compared to the same period last year. “Although there was a 0.7 per cent increase (575 cases compared to EW18/2026), the situation remains under control.

items.title: Govt plans Friday surau in Bukit Bintang for Muslim worshippers
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan said the area currently houses the Oasis Outreach Bukit Bintang centre, which serves as an operations centre. Zulkifli said his team at the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) had actively organised various dakwah outreach and welfare programmes in Bukit Bintang, Chow Kit and surrounding areas over the past two years. "Alhamdulillah, I would like to thank the Federal Territory Islamic Religious Council, Department of Islamic Development Malaysia, Federal Territory Islamic Religious Department and the Federal Territory Mufti Office for their swift action and efforts," he said.

items.title: Police arrest eight, seize ketamine and ecstasy in KL hotel raid linked to a sudden death case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Police arrested eight individuals of various nationalities for alleged drug abuse in a raid at a popular hotel on Jalan Stesen Sentral here this morning. Brickfields police chief ACP Hoo Chang Hook said the raid was conducted following the death of a 31-year-old local man who had been brought from the hotel to the emergency department of Kuala Lumpur Hospital yesterday morning. Hoo said that acting on information obtained, a team from the Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (JSJN) of the Brickfields district police headquarters raided the victim’s hotel room at about 4 am today.

items.title: National Youth Day 2026 draws families and youth with games, music and cultural activities
items.summary: Revathi A. Suntharamurthy, 23, from the Seputeh Hindu Youth Organisation, said the programme stood out for its interactive format and strong focus on youth empowerment. Revathi A. Suntharamurthy said the programme’s interactive approach and emphasis on youth empowerment allowed young people from diverse backgrounds to come together and build connections through shared experiences. — Picture by Raymond Manuel She said it created space for young people from different backgrounds to come together and develop through shared experiences.

items.title: 'Filter of fantasy': Japan trials anime therapy to treat depression
items.summary: AS a teenager struggling to fit into life in rural Sicily, psychiatrist Francesco Panto found refuge in anime, where he discovered characters that resembled the kind of man he wanted to be. Now living in Japan, Panto thinks anime can benefit others and is trialling whether it could be used as a method of therapy, particularly for people who would otherwise struggle to ask for help. "The use of manga and anime supported me so much... they were very important emotional support kind of tools," Panto told AFP.

items.title: Turkiye orders police to evict ousted opposition leadership from headquarters
items.summary: Riot police and crowds gathered outside the gates of CHP headquarters in the Turkish capital after the Ankara governor's office issued the order to remove CHP members aligned with ousted leader Ozgur Ozel. A Turkish appeals court on Thursday annulled the results of a CHP congress at which Ozel was elected in 2023, citing unspecified irregularities. CHP lawmakers on Saturday elected Ozel as leader of the party's parliamentary group.

items.title: MoH: Covid-19 situation in Malaysia remains stable, no deaths recorded
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Covid-19 situation in Malaysia remains stable and continues to show a significant downward trend compared with last year, with no deaths recorded so far this year. The Health Ministry (MoH), in a statement today, said that although neighbouring countries had reported a recent rise in cases, Malaysia recorded a 12.6 per cent drop in infections to 9,682 cases up to Epidemiological Week (EW) 19/2026, compared with the same period last year. It said there was a slight week-on-week increase of 0.7 per cent (575 cases compared with EW18/2026), but the overall situation remained under control.

items.title: MA63 rights still focus of Sabah PKR despite departure of some leaders
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah PKR will continue to pursue the people's rights as enshrined in the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63) despite some leaders choosing to leave the party. Sabah PKR information chief Ahmad Farid Sanuri said the party believed that the process of joining and leaving politics was part and parcel of a mature democratic practice. "Sabah PKR will continue moving forward with a new leadership lineup and an unwavering spirit of struggle for the sake of national political stability.

items.title: Malaysia Airlines receives 200th Boeing aircraft with latest Boeing 737-8 delivery
items.summary: SEPANG, May 24 — Malaysia Airlines today received a new Boeing 737-8 aircraft, marking the 200th Boeing aircraft delivered to the national carrier since 1972. To date, MAG has received 18 Boeing 737-8 aircraft from its total order of 55 Boeing narrowbody aircraft. The order comprises 43 Boeing 737-8 aircraft and 12 Boeing 737-10 aircraft scheduled for delivery through 2030.

items.title: Tengku Hassanal directs Armed Forces to seek Pahang Sultan’s consent for any military drills on state islands
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — The Pahang crown prince has decreed that the Malaysian Armed Forces (ATM) and all relevant agencies must first inform and obtain the Sultan of Pahang’s consent before conducting any military training or exercises in the state. In a statement issued by Istana Abdulaziz in Kuantan and released to the media, Tengku Mahkota Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah said he was paying close attention to the proposed exercises and their potential impact. “His Royal Highness expressed concern that the military activities could disrupt the natural ecosystem, flora and fauna of Pahang,” the statement said.

items.title: Application open June 1 for RM5mil Madani content fund
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Applications for the RM5 million 'Dana Pemangkin Generasi Madani' will officially open from June 1 to 30 to support the production of creative content that reflects the aspirations of Malaysia Madani. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the fund, provided through the National Film Development Corporation Malaysia (FINAS), is open to eligible local companies and youth organisations. Fahmi, who is also Madani Government spokesman, said the fund covers five main production categories, namely telemovies, documentaries, short films, short documentaries and animation.

items.title: Counselling, early intervention keeping domestic‑violence cases under control in Melaka, says state exco
items.summary: MELAKA, May 24 — Counselling sessions and advisory services provided by the National Population and Family Development Board (LPPKN) and the Social Welfare Department (JKM) continue to play an important role in assisting women, especially mothers, who are facing domestic violence and abuse. Melaka Women, Family and Community Development Committee chairman Datuk Kalsom Noordin said more women are now coming forward to seek help and support through the platforms provided by the agencies to resolve domestic conflicts in a safer and more structured manner. She said the counselling sessions not only provide emotional support to victims, but also offer guidance on legal rights, protection and actions that can be taken in cases involving domestic abuse or mistreatment.

items.title: Pivot East to secure high-tech sovereignty
items.summary: THE global race for technological supremacy has given rise to a restrictive narrative wrapped in the language of "dual-use" technologies. Under the guise of international security, the West appear determined to stifle the development of advanced civilian technologies. To view Malaysia's industrial aspirations through a lens of military suspicion is fundamentally absurd.

items.title: [UPDATED] India, US discuss Middle East, trade as US cites progress on Iran conflict
items.summary: NEW DELHI: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio held talks with Indian Foreign Minister Subrahmanyam Jaishankar on Saturday, as the two sides discussed the Middle East, trade, visas, maritime security and energy supplies, while Washington cited progress on efforts to resolve the Iran conflict. Jaishankar said India and the United States had common interests and shared challenges, and that India supported safe maritime passage. He also said India was among the countries with global influence.

items.title: [UPDATED] Govt mulls legal action against Facebook over online scams
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Communications Ministry is considering possible legal action against Facebook for failing to adequately address online scams on its platform. "Maybe we might take legal action (against Facebook). He said MCMC had made 271,472 requests to all platforms to remove harmful content.

items.title: Fire Dept: Two teens feared drowned after being swept away in Sungai Beril
items.summary: DUNGUN, May 24 — Two brothers are feared drowned while swimming with two friends in Sungai Beril, Paka, here today. Paka Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) chief Mohd Rizuan Ramli said Muhammad Amir Ashraf Mohd Zulnaidi, 15, and Muhammad Aidil Amsar Mohd Zulnaidi, 14, from Kampung Kolam here, were believed to have been swept away by strong currents while swimming in the river at about 11 am. Mohd Rizuan said the four students had gone to the river for a swim at about 10.30 am.

items.title: Malaysia urges inclusive framework agreement to secure Strait of Hormuz passage
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has urged parties involved in United States-Iran negotiations to reach an inclusive framework agreement that would strengthen the current ceasefire and ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said such an agreement should also address the legitimate security interests of all nations in the region. Anwar said he had received encouraging indications from leaders in West Asia that negotiations towards a framework agreement between the United States and Iran, including the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, were proceeding on a progressive trajectory.

items.title: Fahmi: Number of fake accounts spreading 3R content on the rise
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Communications Ministry has detected a rise in the use of fake accounts on social media to spread content related to religion, race and royalty (3R), including impersonation of rulers and members of royal families. Its minister, Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, said many of these accounts were created specifically to produce and spread 3R content and once these are spread, the accounts are taken down. "Many fake accounts are being created, particularly to generate 3R content and after the content is produced and disseminated, the accounts are then taken down.

items.title: Gold set to trade cautiously next week amid rising US yields, Middle East tensions
items.summary: Gold and US Treasury bonds are both major safe-haven assets, but they have a distinct inverse relationship whereby when US Treasury bond yields rise, the opportunity cost of holding non-yielding gold increases, putting downward pressure on gold prices. On a week-on-week basis, the spot-month May 2026 contract decreased to US$4,531.60 per troy ounce from US$4,569.90 per troy ounce last Friday, June 2026 fell to U$4,548.60 per troy ounce from US$4,586.90 per troy ounce. The July, August and October 2026 contracts all settled lower at US$4,562.80 per troy ounce from US$4,600.90 per troy ounce in the preceding week.

items.title: Fahmi: Media organisations should be able to earn from social media livestreams, govt to engage TikTok on monetisation rules
items.summary: BUKIT JALIL, May 24 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said Putrajaya is prepared to engage with TikTok over possible monetisation mechanisms for Malaysian media organisations. He said the move followed concerns raised by media industry representatives that their organisations were unable to generate income on some social media platforms, even when their official content attracted significant viewership. Fahmi said the discussions would look at possible solutions for Malaysian media organisations, particularly those with official accounts producing high-performing live content.

items.title: Eight nabbed in hotel raid after man dies at KL Hospital
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police arrested eight people in a hotel raid linked to suspected drug activity following the death of a 31-year-old man brought to Kuala Lumpur Hospital (HKL). Brickfields district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said the man, a local, was pronounced dead at HKL's Emergency Department after being taken from a hotel along Jalan Stesen Sentral. He said a post-mortem had been completed, with the cause of death pending laboratory results.

items.title: Tengku Hassanal: Armed forces must seek Sultan's consent for military drills in Pahang
items.summary: KUANTAN: All military exercises involving the armed forces and related departments conducted in Pahang, including on its islands, must first obtain the consent of the Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah. The Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah Al-Sultan Abdullah, said he was deeply concerned about military training drills planned by the Malaysian Armed Forces, particularly those involving islands within Pahang. "Such military activities could threaten Pahang's environmental ecosystem, including its flora and fauna.

items.title: MCMC to increase public awareness of responsible sharing of videos of crimes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Communications Ministry will intensify digital literacy efforts to raise public awareness on the responsible uploading and sharing of videos involving crimes on social media. Its minister, Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, said the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission will undertake the effort. He said the public must exercise caution when sharing such content, adding that while users are free to post online, they must be mindful that certain materials could interfere with ongoing police investigations.

items.title: Company director in Kota Tinggi loses RM167,050 after responding to fake loan ad on Facebook
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, May 24 — A company director lost RM167,050 after allegedly falling victim to a non-existent loan syndicate. He said the victim was lured by a personal loan advertisement on May 21 offering fast approval, before being instructed to make several payments purportedly for loan processing and approval. Yusof said the victim realised he had been scammed after failing to receive the promised loan and later lodged a police report.

items.title: PN determined to lead Malay-Muslim unity and inter-ethnic harmony narrative, says Samsuri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Perikatan Nasional (PN) is determined to lead the narrative on Malay-Muslim unity and strengthen inter-ethnic harmony as a key condition for national stability and the people's well-being. Its chairman, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar, said Malaysia's unique multi-ethnic society should be celebrated as a strength rather than viewed as a liability. The opposition leader also expressed hope that a national unity movement could be formed to restore harmony and eliminate prejudice within society.

items.title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry deploys four financing agencies to engage MSMEs on the ground, in line with PM’s call
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 24 — The Ministry of Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives (Kuskop) is mobilising four of its financing agencies to go to the ground and engage micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) by providing advisory services and direct financing assistance. Its Minister, Steven Sim, said the four agencies are the National Entrepreneur Group Economic Fund (TEKUN Nasional), Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia (AIM), SME Bank and Bank Rakyat, which are regularly deployed, including during the nationwide Kuskop Madani Sales programme. Yesterday, Anwar called on relevant agencies to intensify efforts to go to the ground and provide information to small and medium enterprises (SMEs) on financing facilities and assistance offered by the government.

items.title: Tens of thousands rally in Serbia demanding elections
items.summary: BELGRADE: Tens of thousands of demonstrators massed in central Belgrade Saturday to renew calls for early elections that grew out of the anti-corruption movement sparked by a deadly rail station disaster. Since the station canopy collapse in November 2024 in Novi Sad, which killed 16 people, calls for a transparent investigation into what happened have snowballed into a push for early polls. Later Saturday, as the rally broke up, clashes broke out between demonstrators and police.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia ready to back peace efforts as US-Iran talks progress
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is ready to support multilateral efforts aimed at achieving lasting peace and stability in the Middle East, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. This comes amid reports of progress in negotiations between the United States and Iran, including discussions on the possible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Anwar said he had received encouraging signals that talks towards a framework agreement between Washington and Tehran were moving forward.

items.title: Abandoned baby girl found at Johor hatch with note naming her Ainul Inaayah
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A baby girl abandoned at the OrphanCare Foundation baby hatch in Taman Pelangi here on March 26 was found with a note bearing the name Ainul Inaayah Abdul Hadi. Johor Baru district welfare officer Khairu Asri Ismail said the note also stated the meaning of the name — "bearer of light" — and indicated the baby was born at 10am on March 22. "The Social Welfare Department received a referral from the Medical Social Work Department at Sultanah Aminah Hospital on March 26.

items.title: Latest Ebola outbreak now third largest in history — is Malaysia at risk?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — The latest Ebola outbreaks in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and Uganda are now the third largest in history, with nearly 750 suspected cases and almost 90 confirmed cases reported within weeks of being declared. The largest Ebola epidemic happened between 2014 and 2016 in West Africa, with 28,600 recorded cases, while the second biggest outbreak also took place in the DRC, where over 3,470 cases were recorded between 2018 and 2020. On May 17, the World Health Organisation declared the outbreak a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC).

items.title: About 30,000 Malaysians expected to visit Thailand next week
items.summary: Thailand-Southeast Asia Halal Trade and Tourism Association president Aida Oujeh said the estimate was based on the projected arrival of Malaysian tourists who were expected to spend about 20,000 baht (RM2,600) each during their stay in southern Thailand. Aida added that a similar number of Indonesian tourists were also expected to enter southern Thailand during the same period. "Most of the tourists are Malaysians visiting Hat Yai, which remains the largest and most popular city in southern Thailand," she said.

items.title: Fahmi announces June 1–30 application window for RM5m Madani content fund, extra RM100,000 for youth programmes
items.summary: BUKIT JALIL, May 24 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today announced that the application for the Madani Generation Catalyst Fund will open from June 1 to June 30, 2026, with RM5 million allocated to support local creative content production. He explained that the fund is open to eligible local companies and youth organisations. “The fund is open to eligible local companies and youth organisations for the production of five key categories which are telefilms, documentaries, short films, short documentaries and animation, with a total allocation of RM5 million,” he said in his speech during the closing ceremony of the Youth Festival at National Youth Day 2026 for the Federal Territories here today.

items.title: US conducts military drill over Venezuelan capital Caracas
items.summary: CARACAS: The US military conducted a drill over the Venezuelan capital Caracas on Saturday, its first military exercise in the South American nation since US troops attacked the capital and captured President Nicolas Maduro and his wife Cilia Flores on Jan 3. The embassy said Francis Donovan, commander of the US Southern Command, which oversees US military operations in the Americas, was present in Caracas. "This keeps us on guard," said Evelyn Rebolledo, 57, an administrator living in the capital.

items.title: Company director loses over RM167k in fake loan scam
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: A company director lost RM167,050 after allegedly falling victim to a non-existent loan scam syndicate. He said the victim, who was promised fast loan approval on May 21, was instructed to make several payments purportedly for processing and approval fees. "The victim made nine transfers into nine different bank accounts in stages to secure the agreed RM500,000 loan," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Quiet Chinese county hit by deadly coal mine disaster
items.summary: COAL miners in the sleepy Chinese county of Qinyuan sometimes dine at Zhang's skewer eatery, especially on payday, so a gas explosion that killed at least 82 of these workers left her feeling sorrow for their bereaved families. The tragedy unfolded in northern Shanxi province, with preliminary findings showing the company operating the mine had committed "serious" violations, state media reported Saturday. The blast caused China's worst mining disaster in 17 years, with search efforts ongoing to find two people still missing, state broadcaster CCTV reported.

items.title: Fahmi wants talks with TikTok to assist media to monetise livestreams
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Communications Ministry plans to hold discussions with TikTok on enabling Malaysian media organisations to monetise their livestream content. He said media outlets should be able to generate revenue from audiences drawn to their official platforms. Speaking to reporters later, Fahmi said the concern extended beyond TikTok, as many social media platforms benefited from content produced by media organisations and official media accounts.

items.title: Kampung Tigot bridge in Sabah reopens after year‑long closure, restoring vital rural access
items.summary: KENINGAU, May 24 — The reopening of the Kampung Tigot bridge after nearly a year of closure has restored a crucial transport link for several rural villages that rely heavily on the route for daily commuting, healthcare, education and economic activities. Kampung Tigot Village Development and Security Committee (JKKK) chairman Basilius Donald said the closure of the bridge had posed significant challenges to residents, who were forced to use alternative routes for almost a year. The damaged old wooden bridge.

items.title: Body of 11-year-old boy reported missing near NKVE found at Sungai Buloh riverbank
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — An 11-year-old boy reported missing near a river by the NKVE close to Menara Finas in Damansara Damai, Petaling Jaya, was found dead this morning. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the boy’s body was found by the riverbank at Jalan Welfare, Kampung Baru Sungai Buloh. The search resumed this morning along the riverbank from Paya Jaras Hilir Bridge to Merbau Sempak Bridge, before the boy was found.

items.title: Nilai police arrest three men, woman over robbery and snatch‑theft cases in Tangkak
items.summary: NILAI, May 24 — Police detained three men and a woman suspected of involvement in robbery and snatch theft cases in Tangkak, Johor, during an operation at Dataran Nilai, Putra Point here yesterday. In a statement, Nilai police chief Supt Johari Yahya said the local suspects, aged between 22 and 28, attempted to flee, resulting in a struggle before they were apprehended. Yesterday, a post on social media platform Threads alleged a shootout between police and criminals at Dataran Nilai, claiming the suspects’ vehicle windows were damaged by gunfire.

items.title: From seaweed to Sabah Tea, state eyes stronger IP push for local products
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 24 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN), through the Intellectual Property Corporation of Malaysia (MyIPO), is developing a comprehensive intellectual property protection mechanism in the state through the Sabah Intellectual Property Development Plan 2026-2030. According to Armizan, the plan will focus on developing a more integrated intellectual property ecosystem, including identifying new Geographical Indications (GIs) for high-value products such as highland agricultural produce, seaweed, and local handicrafts. The achievement places Sabah among the main contributors to the country’s GI portfolio through renowned products such as Sabah Tea, Tenom Coffee, Borneo Virgin Coconut Oil, Sabah Seaweed, Tawau Cocoa, Tambunan Ginger and Sabah Batik.

items.title: Man suspected of stabbing woman in neck near Alor Setar remanded four more days
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 24 — A 32-year-old man suspected of stabbing a woman he knew in the neck inside a car in Titi Gajah, Kepala Batas, near here, has reportedly been remanded for another four days. According to a report in Berita Harian , Magistrate Nur Syifa Mohd Hamzah issued the extension order at the Alor Setar Magistrates’ Court today, after the suspect’s initial seven-day remand expired. Kota Setar police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali reportedly said the remand had been extended until Wednesday to assist investigations.

items.title: Soldier pleads not guilty to abusing son in Kota Bharu
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 24 — A soldier pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of abusing his son two years ago. Zainudin Zainal Abidin, 42, made the plea after the charge was read out before Judge Zulkifli Abllah. — Bernama * If you suspect child abuse, call the following hotlines for free and confidential support: Talian Kasih at 15999 or WhatsApp 019-2615999 (24/7); Talian BuddyBear at 1800-18-2327(BEAR) (noon-midnight daily); and One Crisis Centre (24/7) Wilayah Persekutuan at 03-26155555 (Kuala Lumpur General Hospital), 03-61454333 (Sungai Buloh Hospital) or 03-83124200 (Putrajaya Hospital).

items.title: Victims in online sexual harassment cases urged to prioritise safety, preserve digital evidence, says former Selangor Bar chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Online sexual harassment is becoming an increasingly serious concern, with victims urged to act quickly to preserve digital evidence as crucial material can easily be deleted or altered, said former Selangor Bar chairman Kokila Vaani Vadiveloo. She said Malaysia’s Anti-Sexual Harassment Act 2022 defines sexual harassment as any unwanted conduct of a sexual nature, whether verbal, non-verbal, visual, gestural or physical, with the legal framework increasingly recognising that harassment can also occur in digital spaces. “Online harassment is very much recognised under Malaysian law.

items.title: Welfare Dept records eightfold rise in autism PwD registrations since 2015
items.summary: MELAKA, May 24 — The number of registered persons with disabilities (PwDs) with autism under the Social Welfare Department (JKM) has increased eightfold to 90,459 as of April 30, compared with 2015. JKM director-general Datuk Che Murad Sayang Ramjan said the increase is believed to be due to the effectiveness of autism-related awareness programmes and campaigns among parents, actively carried out by the government through cooperation with various parties, including state governments and the National Autism Society of Malaysia (NASOM) “It is possible that in 2015, many children were already living with autism, but many people still did not understand or were unaware of what autism was until awareness increased following the implementation of various programmes. Che Murad Sayang said the facility was established to help ease the burden on parents in obtaining suitable treatment or therapy for their children.

items.title: Stampin MP urges review after Kuching micro‑businessman faces cash‑flow crisis over RM110 scam‑linked account closures
items.summary: Stampin MP Chong Chieng Jen said the victim, identified only as Liaw, had been running a micro food business for two years and also took online orders. “He also takes orders online. “However, last month, another bank informed him about the closure of his account,” Chong said in a statement.

items.title: Minister proposes gazetting Dayak miring as National Heritage, paving way for Unesco bid
items.summary: SIBU, May 24 — The National Unity Ministry has proposed that the traditional Dayak ceremony, namely ‘miring’, be gazetted as National Heritage. Therefore, he said the Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture (MOTAC), through the National Heritage Department, needs to gazette the ceremony as a national heritage first so that it can be documented, preserved and promoted more comprehensively. The ceremony, held to welcome the upcoming Gawai festival, was jointly organised by Sarakup Indu Dayak Sarawak (SIDS) Sibu, Sarawak Dayak National Union (SDNU) Sibu and Orang Ulu National Association (OUNA) Sibu.

items.title: Police: Man found dead in Proton Wira in Baling had 20 criminal, drug records
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — A man found dead inside a locked car near a Chinese cemetery in Simpang Tiga Tawar, Baling, yesterday had 20 previous criminal and narcotics records, police reportedly said. According to Sinar Harian , Baling police chief Superintendent Brandon Anak Richard Joe said the man was a Malaysian in his 30s. Police have reportedly seized the car for further investigations.

items.title: MOE probing alleged sexual misconduct involving teacher in Setiu
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 24 — An alleged sexual misconduct case involving a teacher at a school in Setiu, which went viral on social media, is being investigated by the Integrity Unit of the Ministry of Education (MOE). The Terengganu Education Department (JPNT) said that investigations are ongoing and the public is urged to channel any information to the department. Action will be taken against anyone found guilty,” the department said in a statement.

items.title: ‘We need the president’: Perak PKR urges Anwar to defend Tambun in next general election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Perak PKR has reportedly asked Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to defend Tambun in the next general election, saying his presence remains important to the party’s campaign in the state. However, Perak PKR chairman Mohamad Hairul Amir Sabri said there had been no discussion so far on whether Anwar would switch seats, according to a report in The Star . “We in Perak very much need the president to remain in Perak to help us win more parliamentary and state seats in the next election,” he was quoted as saying.

items.title: Ewon: RM16.8m Timpangoh–Babagon Bridge completed over three months ahead of schedule
items.summary: PENAMPANG, May 24 — The RM16.8 million Timpangoh-Babagon Bridge project has been completed more than three months ahead of schedule, providing a major boost to economic activities and connectivity for residents in Mukim Babagon. He said the completion of the bridge would significantly improve economic activities in the area, particularly for agricultural communities. In another development, Ewon said SMK Kipovo in Kampung Babagon is expected to begin operations next year.

items.title: From postal hub to cultural space: The story of KL’s old General Post Office
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Dataran Merdeka boasts many historical elements, but one of the most eye-catching architectural buildings is the old General Post Office (GPO), which evokes a deep history in Malaysia’s postal system. Situated in the heart of Kuala Lumpur’s heritage area, the GPO stands strong at the intersection of Jalan Hishamuddin and Jalan Raja, just a stone’s throw away from the iconic Sultan Abdul Samad Building. Opened in 1907, the GPO is one of the most spectacular post offices ever built in Malaysia.
